📈 Performance Logs

📊 Performance Metrics Guide

⏱️ Latency (ms) - "The Delay"
Time for data to travel from source to destination and back (ping). Lower is better (under 50ms = responsive). High latency causes delays.
✓ Good: <50ms | ⚠️ Fair: 50-200ms | ❌ Poor: >200ms
🚀 Throughput (Mbps) - "Actual Speed"
Actual data successfully transferred over network. Higher is better. Affected by congestion and packet loss.
✓ Good: >2Mbps | ⚠️ Fair: 1-2Mbps | ❌ Poor: <1Mbps
📦 Packet Loss (%) - "Missing Data"
Percentage of data packets that fail to reach destination. Caused by congestion, faulty hardware, weak signals.
✓ Good: 0% | ⚠️ Fair: 1-2% | ❌ Poor: >2%
〰️ Jitter (ms) - "Stability/Inconsistency"
Variation in packet arrival times. Measures latency fluctuation. High jitter causes choppy audio/video.
✓ Good: <10ms | ⚠️ Fair: 10-30ms | ❌ Poor: >30ms
